description:
You are acting as a release-manager who is finishing today’s roll-out of a web application that lives in:

/home/user/deployment

The directory tree is already partially prepared for you:

/home/user/deployment  
└── releases  
    ├── v1.0.0  
    └── v1.1.0  
/home/user/deployment/current  →  /home/user/deployment/releases/v1.1.0

Your job is to:

1. Create a new release directory named v1.2.0 under /home/user/deployment/releases.  
   • The directory may stay empty or contain any placeholder file(s); the tests only verify that the directory itself exists.  

2. Repoint the symbolic link /home/user/deployment/current so that, when you list it with `ls -l /home/user/deployment/current`, it shows:
   `/home/user/deployment/current -> /home/user/deployment/releases/v1.2.0`

3. Record the deployment in the log file /home/user/deployment/deploy.log.  
   • The log file must exist when you are done.  
   • It must contain exactly ONE line, ending with a newline character (`\n`).  
   • Format (strictly, spaces matter, no extra characters):  

     YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ – v1.2.0 – current→/home/user/deployment/releases/v1.2.0  

     Example (the timestamp you write must be the real current UTC time when you run the command, not the example timestamp):  
     2023-05-09T14:37:02Z – v1.2.0 – current→/home/user/deployment/releases/v1.2.0  

   • Note the en-dash (U+2013) between columns, and the arrow “current→/home/...”. Use exactly that arrow character (U+2192).  
   • The line must end with a single newline, and the file must contain no other lines.  

The automated tests will verify:

• /home/user/deployment/releases/v1.2.0 exists and is a directory.  
• /home/user/deployment/current is a symbolic link that resolves to /home/user/deployment/releases/v1.2.0 (not a copy).  
• /home/user/deployment/deploy.log exists, contains exactly one line, and that line matches the required format, including the correct absolute destination path.  

Accomplishing these three steps should only require three or four standard shell commands (mkdir, ln, echo, date, etc.). You do not need sudo or root privileges, and everything can be done as the regular “user”.
truth:
Initial state supplied by the test harness BEFORE the agent starts:

1. Directory layout:
   /home/user/deployment/
   ├── releases/
   │   ├── v1.0.0/              # Empty directory
   │   └── v1.1.0/              # Empty directory
   └── current (symlink → /home/user/deployment/releases/v1.1.0)

2. No directory named /home/user/deployment/releases/v1.2.0 exists yet.

3. /home/user/deployment/deploy.log either does not exist or is an empty file.

Required final state used for automated verification AFTER the agent finishes:

1. A directory:
   /home/user/deployment/releases/v1.2.0    (type: directory; permissions 755 or default umask)

2. A symbolic link:
   /home/user/deployment/current → /home/user/deployment/releases/v1.2.0
   • The link must not be removed and recreated as a *regular* directory or file; it must be a symlink whose target path exactly matches the above string.

3. A file:
   /home/user/deployment/deploy.log
   • Must contain exactly ONE line terminated by '\n'.
   • Regex for the entire line (POSIX-extended, anchors included):

     ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z – v1\.2\.0 – current→/home/user/deployment/releases/v1\.2\.0\n$

   • File size must equal the length of that single line plus one newline (no extra whitespace or blank lines).

No other files, directories, or symlinks may be altered inside /home/user/deployment; specifically, the two older release directories must remain untouched.
